feed.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero

entry.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
entry.summary: In a thoughtful analysis shared via an online post, the concept of implementing a floor fee is explored as a potentially viable method due to its compatibility with soft-forking. However, it is suggested that a more definitive solution to the issue at hand could involve the combination of tail emission alongside an EIP-1559 style fee burn and priority tipping. This approach is deemed to possibly offer a balanced resolution by addressing various aspects of transaction fees and network sustainability in blockchain technology. For a detailed examination of these ideas, you can view the original content [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/addressing-the-diminishing-block-subsidy/2640).
